title: Record a dated repository-grounded cost baseline
description: Maintain the resource inventory, pricing model, scale-to-zero review, and rollout procedure alongside the infrastructure.
rationale: A quantified baseline makes fixed-cost regressions and environment multipliers reviewable before deployment.
acceptance_criteria: The model names its region, price date, usage assumptions, fixed floor, variable cost, and account-wide free-tier caveats.
The inventory agrees with the current Terraform resources.
No estimate is represented as a live bill or applied Terraform plan.
rollback_notes: Revert documentation only if it becomes inaccurate, replacing it in the same change with a newly dated model rather than deleting the cost baseline.

title: Prove CloudFront flat-rate Free plan compatibility in dev
description: After deployment, verify paid-account eligibility and test whether the existing CloudFront distribution, S3 origin, API origin, origin-verification header, and exactly five Terraform-managed WAF rules retain their behavior when associated with the $0/month plan.
rationale: The plan can cover the current $10/month WAF floor, but Free has no uptime SLA and billing-plan ownership must not silently replace security controls.
acceptance_criteria: The account is eligible for the Free plan and the distribution remains under 1 million allowed requests and 100 GB transfer per month.
All five existing WAF protections remain enabled with unchanged block/count behavior.
CloudFront still serves the private S3 origin and forwards API cookies, Origin, content type, and CSRF headers correctly.
Direct API Gateway access without the origin-verification header is still rejected.
The operator explicitly accepts that the Free plan has no uptime SLA.
A tested path returns the distribution to pay-as-you-go pricing.
rollback_notes: Switch the dev distribution back to pay-as-you-go pricing, confirm the Terraform-managed WAF ACL is associated, rerun smoke tests, and do not attempt production enrollment if security behavior or Terraform ownership drifts.

title: Keep dev ephemeral instead of continuously deployed
description: Define an explicit create-test-destroy workflow for dev while retaining encrypted remote state and reproducible deployment artifacts.
rationale: A continuously deployed dev stack adds roughly $15/month, far more than the stated production workload adds.
acceptance_criteria: Dev can be recreated from protected configuration without manual resource repair.
Destroy requires protected-environment approval and an explicit typed confirmation.
No real personal data or production backup depends on dev.
Remote state and state KMS resources are not destroyed with the dev application.
rollback_notes: Redeploy dev from the last green commit using the protected dev environment and run migrations plus fictional-data smoke tests.

title: Tune the production budget to the measured baseline
description: After one complete billing month, set the production monthly budget near $50 and add thresholds appropriate to the measured $16-$18 baseline.
rationale: The current $300 threshold is too high to detect an unpaused database, accidental second environment, or inference abuse early.
acceptance_criteria: A clean full-month Cost Explorer or CUR baseline has been reviewed.
The threshold includes expected KMS rotation growth and intentional deployment events.
The alert recipient confirms receipt in production.
The budget remains notification-only unless a separately reviewed action is approved.
rollback_notes: Restore the previous budget limit if alerts are noisy because this account includes unrelated workloads; do not disable billing visibility.

title: Guard against high-cardinality CloudWatch metrics
description: Extend the repository safety scan to reject personal, request-derived, or otherwise unbounded EMF dimensions and require fixed operation names.
rationale: CloudWatch charges each unique dimension combination as a metric; an unbounded dimension can make observability the fastest-growing variable cost.
acceptance_criteria: Only Environment, Service, and Operation are emitted as dimensions.
Tests reject unsafe metric metadata such as an email-derived operation value.
Existing metrics and alarm names remain unchanged.
rollback_notes: Revert the scan/test change if it blocks a legitimate bounded operation, but retain the fixed dimension schema and add the operation to an explicit allowlist instead.
